title: select_with_fee always assumes 2 outputs; builder sees 1 output when change==0
description: tx_builder.rs select_with_fee computes fee using sapling_logical_actions(n, 2) (2 outputs assumed). When change == 0, build_shielded_tx adds only 1 output. FeeRule::standard() in builder.build() then computes fee for (n spends, 1 output). For n <= 2: max(n, 2) == max(n, 1) so fees agree. For n >= 3 spends and change == 0: our fee = 5000*n (from max(n,2)=n), builder fee = 5000*max(n,1) = 5000*n — same. Conclusion: the values happen to match for all plausible inputs because max(n, 2) == max(n, 1) when n >= 2. The assumption is not documented and could break if n_outputs is changed from 2 without updating both the selection and the actual output count. Not currently broken but the implicit coupling is a latent defect.
close_reason: Documented: added 'Output count assumption' comment to select_with_fee explaining that 2 vs 1 output produces identical fees due to GRACE_ACTIONS=2 floor, and what must be re-verified if the fee formula changes.
